title: Chromolaena myriocephala
intro: Chromolaena myriocephala, also known as tree aster or bitter tree, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena myriocephala include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chromolaena myriocephala include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Chromolaena myriocephala is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant belongs to the genus Chromolaena, which contains about 100 species of flowering plants native to tropical regions of the Americas. The genus Chromolaena is placed in the tribe Eupatorieae, a group of flowering plants that also includes the genera Eupatorium and Ageratina. Chromolaena myriocephala is specifically an annual or perennial herbaceous plant with a wide distribution across the Americas. It is a member of the subfamily Asteroideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies of the Asteraceae family.
